summary: First Peach decompilation cleanup/matching pass. The PR matched one function, ftPeach_8011BA20, moved it from asm into src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftpeach.c, added the needed ftCommonData field at offset 0x70, and systematically renamed recognized Peach asm function boundaries from generic func_/lbl_ names to ftPeach_ addresses. It also removed unreferenced Peach function and float-label declarations from the global include stubs, renamed many ftPeach asm float labels to f32_804D97xx style, and updated action-state table references and callsites to the newly recognized ftPeach_ names.
what_changed: Matched ftPeach_8011BA20 in C. The function returns true unless Peach's stick Y input is below p_ftCommonData->x70_someLStickYMax and input.x65C does not have mask 0xC00 set.
Removed the original asm body for func_8011BA20 from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpeach.s and updated asm callers to branch to ftPeach_8011BA20.
Added prototype s32 ftPeach_8011BA20(Fighter* fp) to ftpeach.h.
Changed ftpeach.h from a traditional include guard to #pragma once.
Split ftCommonData filler in fighter.h to expose /* 0x70 */ f32 x70_someLStickYMax, needed by the matched Peach function.
Renamed many recognized Peach function entry labels in ftpeach.s from func_8011xxxx or lbl_8011xxxx to ftPeach_8011xxxx.
Updated internal branch-and-link callsites and action-state table entries to use the ftPeach_8011xxxx names instead of generic labels.
Renamed Peach-local floating constants from lbl_804D97xx-style names to f32_804D97xx-style names in asm references.
Removed a block of unreferenced Peach declarations from include/functions.h and include/variables.h.
Updated existing C calls in ftPeach_8011B704 from func_8011D598, func_8011E2E8, and func_8011CFA0 to ftPeach_8011D598, ftPeach_8011E2E8, and ftPeach_8011CFA0.
smart_moves: Even though only one function was matched, the author cleaned the surrounding asm namespace, which reduces future ambiguity about where Peach functions start and which labels are merely local branches.
Removing stale global stubs from include/functions.h and include/variables.h kept the global unknown-symbol lists from accumulating Peach-specific noise after symbols were recognized or localized.
Giving float constants f32_ names made their type/use clearer than raw lbl_ names while preserving exact addresses for matching.
The ftCommonData filler was split only around the needed discovered offset, preserving the rest of the unknown layout while enabling typed C access at 0x70.
Action-state table pointers were updated together with function label renames, avoiding a half-renamed state where tables still pointed at lbl_ names.
decomp_lessons: A useful first pass for a character file can be mostly preparatory: identify function boundaries, rename recognized functions, remove unreferenced globals, and match the smallest confidently understood function.
When matching a small function that reads an unknown common-data field, expose only the specific struct offset needed rather than over-claiming nearby fields.
Do not leave recognized function entry points as lbl_ labels; promote them to module-prefixed symbols so action-state tables and callsites become searchable.
After renaming asm symbols, update all dependent tables and cross-file references in the same PR to keep symbol names consistent.
For constants loaded with lfs, f32_804Dxxxx names are more informative than lbl_804Dxxxx and help distinguish typed float constants from code labels or opaque data.
naming_conventions: Peach functions use the prefix ftPeach_ followed by the original address, e.g. ftPeach_8011BA20, ftPeach_8011BB6C, ftPeach_8011D598.
Recognized function starts should be renamed from func_8011xxxx or lbl_8011xxxx to ftPeach_8011xxxx when the function boundary is known but semantic name is not yet known.
Float constants in the Peach rodata/sdata area were renamed from lbl_804D97xx to f32_804D97xx.
Newly identified ftCommonData field at offset 0x70 was named x70_someLStickYMax, preserving offset-first naming while adding a tentative semantic hint.
ftpeach.h was modernized to use #pragma once rather than _ftpeach_h_ include guards.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The matched C for ftPeach_8011BA20 uses a default result = 1 and a negated compound condition to reproduce the asm control flow around the fcmpo/cror comparison and input bitmask test.
The input bitmask from fp->input.x65C was represented as 0xC00, matching the asm rlwinm condition over bits 0x14-0x15.
The stick comparison was expressed as fp->input.x624_lstick_y >= p_ftCommonData->x70_someLStickYMax to match the original floating-point compare behavior.
Function-boundary recognition in asm was applied broadly before full decompilation, turning table-referenced labels such as lbl_8011BC38 and lbl_8011BF88 into ftPeach_ symbols.
Maintained address-suffixed names rather than guessing behavior names where semantics were not yet proven.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that give semantic names to ftPeach_8011BA20 or x70_someLStickYMax.
Look for subsequent Peach passes that decompile ftPeach_8011BB6C, ftPeach_8011BD6C, ftPeach_8011BE80, or ftPeach_8011BF34.
Check whether the f32_804D97xx constants were later moved to typed C rodata or given semantic names.
Search for review or later cleanup around #pragma once adoption in character headers.
Compare later ftCommonData layout changes around offset 0x70 to validate or rename x70_someLStickYMax.
